options: A. England beat Australia at Lord's
B. Australia beat England by exactly 45 runs — the same margin as the first Test
C. The match was drawn in a fitting tribute
D. Don Bradman played his last match
explanation: The remarkable coincidence of the Centenary Test (MCG, 1977) was that Australia defeated England by exactly 45 runs, the same margin as in the first Test 100 years earlier. Derek Randall scored 174 for England.

question: How many sixes did Kieron Pollard hit in an over off Akila Dananjaya in a T20I in 2021?
explanation: Kieron Pollard of West Indies hit six sixes in one over off Sri Lankan spinner Akila Dananjaya in a T20I in Antigua in March 2021, joining Yuvraj Singh as the only players to achieve this in T20Is.

question: Who has the most appearances as captain in Test cricket?
explanation: Graeme Smith of South Africa captained his team in 109 Tests, the most by any player in Test cricket history, winning 53 of them.

question: Who is the only Test cricketer to score 10,000 runs and take 400 wickets?
explanation: While several players have been great all-rounders, the only player to reach both 10,000 runs and 400 wickets in first-class or international cricket as an all-rounder would need checking. Kapil Dev was the first to 400 Test wickets AND scored 5,248 Test runs. This is a deliberately tricky question.

question: What was Kapil Dev's bowling action classified as?
explanation: Kapil Dev was a right-arm fast-medium bowler who generated significant outswing and was one of India's greatest all-rounders, taking 434 Test wickets.

question: Who scored the most Test runs in the 2024 year?
explanation: Yashasvi Jaiswal of India had an outstanding 2024 in Test cricket, scoring over 1,400 Test runs during the calendar year, including a stunning 2024-25 home series.

question: What is the record for the most runs scored in a single T20I by a team?
options: B. 260/5 by Australia vs Sri Lanka
D. 258/4 by India vs Sri Lanka
explanation: Afghanistan scored 278/3 against Ireland in Dehradun in February 2019, with Hazratullah Zazai scoring 162*, the highest team total in T20 International cricket.

question: What is unique about Steve Smith's batting technique?
options: A. He bats left-handed but throws right-handed
B. He has an unorthodox trigger movement to off stump before delivery
C. He uses an extremely heavy bat of over 3lbs
D. He always waits outside the crease
explanation: Steve Smith's batting is distinctive for his exaggerated trigger movement, where he steps toward off stump just before the ball is bowled, giving him unusual balance and a unique attacking angle.

question: Who is the only player to have scored centuries against all 10 Test-playing nations at the time?
explanation: Sachin Tendulkar is the only batsman to have scored Test centuries against all 10 Test-playing nations that existed during his career — an extraordinary measure of his consistency.

question: Which team holds the record for the highest score in a T20I innings?
explanation: Afghanistan scored 278/3 against Ireland in 2019, the highest team total in T20 International cricket history, fueled by Hazratullah Zazai's 162*.

question: What major record did Rohit Sharma set in the 2019 ICC ODI World Cup?
options: A. Most sixes in a World Cup
B. Most centuries in a single World Cup edition (5 centuries)
C. Fastest century in a World Cup
D. Most runs in a World Cup match
explanation: Rohit Sharma scored five centuries in the 2019 ICC ODI World Cup, the most by any batsman in a single World Cup edition. He scored 648 runs in the tournament.

question: Who was the first cricketer to score 10,000 ODI runs?
explanation: Sachin Tendulkar was the first cricketer to score 10,000 runs in ODI cricket, achieving this milestone in 2001. He eventually finished with 18,426 ODI runs.

question: How many T20 World Cup titles has West Indies won?
explanation: West Indies have won the ICC T20 World Cup twice, in 2012 and 2016, with Carlos Brathwaite's four sixes off Ben Stokes in the 2016 final being one of cricket's most dramatic moments.

question: What is the record for the most runs conceded by a bowler in a Test innings?
explanation: LO Fleetwood-Smith of Australia conceded 298 runs in England's mammoth 903/7d at The Oval in 1938, the most runs conceded by any bowler in a single Test innings.

question: Which team became the first to lose a Test match after enforcing a follow-on?
explanation: India in 2001 became only the third team in history to win a Test after following on. Australia won that Kolkata match in 1981 after England followed on. Actually, Australia are the ones who lost in the 2001 Kolkata Test after enforcing the follow-on. This is the correct answer.

question: Which partnership record did Mahela Jayawardene and Kumar Sangakkara set for Sri Lanka?
options: A. 576 for the 3rd wicket vs India
B. 624 for the 3rd wicket vs South Africa
C. 399 for the 1st wicket vs Bangladesh
D. 521 for the 2nd wicket vs Zimbabwe
explanation: Mahela Jayawardene (374) and Kumar Sangakkara (287) set the world record Test partnership of 624 for the 3rd wicket against South Africa in Colombo in 2006.

question: What was the highest total scored in a Test by Sri Lanka?
explanation: Sri Lanka scored 952/6 declared against India in Colombo in 1997, the highest team total in Test cricket history, surpassing England's 903/7d from 1938.

question: What is the name of the annual Test match played between India and Pakistan at a neutral venue?
options: B. There is no regular neutral venue series — they play bilateral series at home/away or ICC events
explanation: India and Pakistan have not played bilateral Test cricket since 2007 due to political tensions. Their matches now only occur at ICC events (World Cups, Champions Trophy, Asia Cup). There is no named regular series.

question: Who was the first player to score a century in each innings of a Test match?
explanation: Warren Bardsley of Australia became the first player to score a century in both innings of a Test match, doing so at The Oval in 1909.

question: What is 'bat-pad' in cricket fielding terminology?
options: A. A fielder positioned close on the leg side to catch balls that pop off bat and pad
B. A batting glove that combines bat and hand protection
C. The technique of playing off the pad deliberately
D. When the ball hits both bat and pad simultaneously for a dismissal
explanation: Bat-pad is a close fielding position (usually on the leg side, near the batsman) where a fielder is positioned to catch balls that pop up off the bat and pad when a batsman plays defensively.

question: Which bowler holds the record for the most wickets in T20 World Cup history?
explanation: Lasith Malinga holds the record for most wickets in T20 World Cup history, taking over 38 wickets across multiple tournament editions.

question: Who scored the first century in T20 International cricket for England?
explanation: Kevin Pietersen scored the first T20I century for England, scoring 115 against South Africa in Johannesburg in 2010 in a world record T20I innings at the time.

question: What is the current ICC rule on the number of bouncers per over?
explanation: Under current ICC rules, a bowler is allowed a maximum of 2 short-pitched deliveries (bouncers) per over in all formats of international cricket.

question: Who scored the highest individual score in a Test match at Lord's?
explanation: Graham Gooch scored 333 runs for England against India at Lord's in 1990, the highest individual score in a Test match at Lord's Cricket Ground.

question: What does 'WADA' compliance mean in cricket?
options: A. World Anti-Doping Agency — players must follow doping regulations
B. Wicket-keeper's Association and Delivery Analytics
explanation: WADA (World Anti-Doping Agency) compliance means that cricketers and cricket boards adhere to global anti-doping regulations, including random testing and whereabouts requirements.

question: Who was the captain of India when they toured England for the first time in 1932?
explanation: CK Nayudu captained India on their inaugural tour of England in 1932, leading them in their first-ever Test match at Lord's. He is considered one of India's earliest cricket legends.

question: What unique dismissal mode was used for the first time in the 2023 ODI World Cup?
explanation: Angelo Mathews of Sri Lanka became the first batsman ever dismissed 'timed out' in international cricket during the 2023 ODI World Cup against Bangladesh. He wasn't ready to face within the required 2 minutes.

question: What scoring milestone did Rohit Sharma reach in the 2023 ODI World Cup?
options: B. Became fastest to 10,000 ODI runs
D. Broke the record for most sixes in ODI World Cup history
explanation: Rohit Sharma broke the record for most sixes hit in ODI World Cup history during the 2023 tournament, adding to his existing record as the most prolific six-hitter in ODI cricket.

question: What percentage of Tests result in draws, wins, or losses (approximately, as of 2025)?
explanation: Historically, approximately 30% of Test matches end in draws with 70% producing results. The introduction of day-night Tests and more aggressive batting (Bazball era) has reduced the draw percentage in recent years.

question: What is the 'Box' in cricket terminology?
options: A. The restricted area outside which 5 fielders must stand
B. A protective guard worn by batsmen in the groin area
C. The square on the field where the pitch is prepared
D. The designated area for reserved fielders
explanation: In cricket, 'the box' is a protective guard worn by male batsmen and wicket-keepers to protect the groin area from impact by the ball.

question: What was the first country to beat Australia in a Test series in Australia in the 21st century?
explanation: India became the first Asian team to win a Test series in Australia when they won 2-1 in 2018-19, under captain Virat Kohli. It was a historic result.

question: Who took the most wickets in the 2025 ICC Champions Trophy?
explanation: Mohammed Shami was one of India's key performers in the 2025 ICC Champions Trophy, being the tournament's leading wicket-taker as India won the title.

question: Which player has the best career bowling economy rate in T20I cricket (minimum 50 wickets)?
explanation: Rashid Khan of Afghanistan has maintained one of the best economy rates in T20I cricket history with well over 50 wickets, regularly conceding fewer than 6 runs per over.

question: Who holds the record for the most Test wins as a captain?
explanation: Graeme Smith of South Africa holds the record for the most Test wins as a captain with 53 victories from 109 matches as skipper.

question: What is the current ODI batting record for most consecutive 50+ scores?
explanation: Kumar Sangakkara scored centuries in 4 consecutive innings in the 2015 ODI World Cup and also has outstanding records for consecutive fifty-plus scores in ODIs.

question: What is the term for the fielding position at the back of the leg side, near the boundary?
explanation: Fine leg is a fielding position on the leg side, close to the boundary behind the wicket-keeper. It is designed to stop fine deflections off the edge or the pad.

question: Who scored four consecutive centuries at the 2015 ODI World Cup?
explanation: Kumar Sangakkara of Sri Lanka scored centuries in four consecutive innings at the 2015 ODI World Cup — 105 vs Bangladesh, 117* vs England, 124 vs Australia, and 111 vs Scotland.

question: Who has taken the most wickets in Test cricket among pace bowlers?
explanation: James Anderson of England is the leading wicket-taker among pace bowlers in Test cricket, having taken over 700 Test wickets before his retirement in 2024.

question: What is the fastest fifty in Test cricket history?
explanation: David Warner of Australia scored the fastest fifty in Test cricket history off just 17 balls against Pakistan at the SCG in January 2017.

question: What was notable about the 2019 Ashes series in terms of individual performances?
options: A. Jofra Archer made his debut and bowled at 96 mph
B. Ben Stokes was the only consistent English performer throughout the series
C. Steve Smith scored 774 runs in 7 innings despite being banned for ball-tampering
D. Steve Smith scored 774 runs in the series returning from his ball-tampering ban
explanation: Steve Smith scored 774 runs in the 2019 Ashes series in England in his comeback after serving a year's ban for the Sandpaper Gate scandal, dominating England's bowling attack.

question: How many runs did Don Bradman average per Test innings?
explanation: Don Bradman's Test batting average of 99.94 is considered the greatest individual statistical achievement in any team sport. He scored 6,996 runs in 80 innings with 10 not outs.

question: What is the record for the most ducks by a batsman in Test cricket?
explanation: Courtney Walsh of West Indies scored the most ducks in Test cricket history with 30 ducks. As a tail-ender who batted for 17 years, this is not surprising given his role as a specialist bowler.

question: What was the crucial innings played by VVS Laxman in the famous 2001 Kolkata Test?
explanation: VVS Laxman scored 281 in India's second innings in the 2001 Kolkata Test, combined with Rahul Dravid's 180, helping India win by 171 runs after following on — one of cricket's greatest comebacks.

question: Which team has the best overall win/loss ratio in Test cricket history?
explanation: Australia has historically the best win/loss ratio in Test cricket, driven by their dominant periods in the 1990s and 2000s under Steve Waugh and Ricky Ponting.

question: In which year was the last 6-ball over changed from 8-ball overs in Australia?
explanation: Australia used 8-ball overs in Test cricket until 1979-80 when they switched to 6-ball overs to align with the rest of the world. Various over lengths were tried historically in different countries.

question: Who has the most T20 International appearances?
explanation: Shoaib Malik of Pakistan holds the record for the most T20 International appearances, with well over 120 matches played across his long career.

question: What happened in the 2016 ICC T20 World Cup final between England and West Indies?
options: B. West Indies won with Carlos Brathwaite hitting 4 sixes in the last over
C. Match was tied and West Indies won the super over
D. West Indies won by 10 wickets
explanation: In the 2016 T20 World Cup final, West Indies needed 19 off the last over. Carlos Brathwaite hit 4 consecutive sixes off Ben Stokes to win the match and title for West Indies.

question: What is the highest test score by an opener in a Test match?
explanation: Len Hutton opened for England and scored 364 against Australia at The Oval in 1938, a record that stood for 20 years. It remains the highest score by an opener in Test cricket.

question: What is 'Powerplay 2' (PP2) in ODI cricket?
options: A. Overs 11-40, with maximum 4 fielders outside circle
C. A second batting powerplay between overs 11-40 taken by the batting team
D. No such thing in current ODI rules
explanation: The batting Powerplay was abolished from ODI cricket. The current ODI fielding restrictions are: PP1 (overs 1-10: max 2 outside 30-yard circle), then overs 11-40 (max 4 outside), and overs 41-50 (max 5 outside).

question: Which stadium hosted the 2023 ICC ODI World Cup final?
explanation: The 2023 ICC ODI World Cup final between India and Australia was held at the Narendra Modi Stadium in Ahmedabad, India. Australia won the match.

question: Who is the only player to score a century on T20I debut?
options: D. Several players have done this
explanation: Multiple players have scored centuries on T20I debut. This is a trick question — it's not exclusive to one player as T20I cricket has seen explosive debut innings from several batters over the years.

question: Who took the most wickets in the history of the Ashes series?
explanation: Shane Warne took 195 wickets in Ashes cricket, the most by any bowler in the history of the England vs Australia Test series.

question: What is the term for the scoring method used when a fielder's throw from the outfield hits the stumps directly and dismisses a batsman?
explanation: A direct hit run-out occurs when a fielder throws the ball and it directly hits the stumps, with the batsman out of their crease. It is one of the most spectacular fielding dismissals.

question: How many times has Australia won the ICC ODI World Cup?
explanation: Australia has won the ODI World Cup six times: 1987, 1999, 2003, 2007, 2015, and 2023, making them the most successful team in World Cup history.

question: What is the record for the fastest delivery ever bowled by a female cricketer in international cricket?
explanation: Cathryn Fitzpatrick of Australia is credited with bowling one of the fastest deliveries in women's cricket at approximately 125 km/h, making her a pioneering fast bowler in the women's game.

question: What happened in the famous 1979 World Cup final?
options: A. West Indies beat England by 92 runs
B. West Indies beat Australia by 17 runs
C. England beat West Indies by 2 wickets
D. West Indies beat Pakistan by 8 wickets
explanation: West Indies beat England by 92 runs in the 1979 World Cup final at Lord's, with Viv Richards scoring a brilliant 138 not out to seal a dominant victory.

question: Which country does Trent Boult represent?
explanation: Trent Boult is a New Zealand left-arm fast bowler, known for his ability to swing the ball and take wickets in all conditions. He retired from international cricket to pursue franchise cricket.

question: What is the world record for the highest score in a women's Test cricket innings?
explanation: Kiran Baluch of Pakistan scored 242 against the West Indies in 2004, the highest individual score in a Women's Test match, a record that stands to this day.

question: Which team has never qualified for the final of the ICC ODI World Cup despite consistently strong performances?
explanation: South Africa are famously known as 'chokers' in World Cup cricket. Despite their consistent strength, they have never reached an ODI World Cup final and have been eliminated in semi-finals under dramatic circumstances multiple times.

question: What is the record for most wickets taken in ICC T20 World Cup history by a spinner?
explanation: Shakib Al Hasan of Bangladesh holds the record for the most wickets by a spinner in T20 World Cup history, making him one of the tournament's most consistent bowling performers.

question: In what year did Afghanistan make their Test cricket debut?
explanation: Afghanistan played their first Test match in 2018 against India in Bengaluru. They were granted Test status by the ICC in 2017 along with Ireland.

question: Who was the first player to be dismissed by the 'obstructing the field' dismissal in a major international match?
explanation: Len Hutton was dismissed for obstructing the field against South Africa at The Oval in 1951, one of the earliest recorded instances of this rare dismissal in international cricket.

question: What is the highest total chased to win a T20I match?
options: B. 258 by Australia vs New Zealand
C. 266/5 by New Zealand vs Sri Lanka
explanation: New Zealand successfully chased 266/5 against Sri Lanka in 2016 in Christchurch, the highest successful chase in T20 International cricket history.

question: Who has the best bowling figures in a single T20I innings?
explanation: Deepak Chahar of India has the best figures in a single T20I innings with 6/7 against Bangladesh in Nagpur in November 2019, the best bowling figures in T20I cricket history.

question: What is the record for the highest team total in a T20 World Cup match?
options: A. 260/6 by Sri Lanka vs Kenya 2007
C. 245/6 by West Indies vs India 2016
explanation: Sri Lanka scored 260/6 against Kenya in the inaugural 2007 ICC T20 World Cup, which remains the highest team total in T20 World Cup match history.

question: Who made the most appearances in the ICC Champions Trophy across all editions?
explanation: Mahela Jayawardene appeared in the most Champions Trophy matches across the tournament's history, representing Sri Lanka in multiple editions from 2000 to 2013.

question: What is a 'sticky wicket' in cricket?
options: A. A pitch that has become wet and then baked in sun, making it difficult to bat on
B. A pitch with too much grass
C. A wicket-keeper that never drops catches
D. A pitch covered in artificial surface
explanation: A sticky wicket (or sticky dog) is a pitch that has become wet and then dries rapidly in sunshine, making it extremely difficult to bat on as the ball behaves unpredictably.

question: Who is the only player to have scored a Test century on debut in all four innings of their first two Tests?
options: C. No player has done this
explanation: No player has scored Test centuries in all four innings of their first two Tests. Lawrence Rowe scored hundreds in both innings of his debut Test, but subsequent performances didn't extend to all four innings over two Tests.

question: What was Don Bradman's average in Ashes cricket specifically (vs England only)?
explanation: Don Bradman averaged approximately 89 runs per innings in Ashes cricket specifically, slightly lower than his overall 99.94 because the Bodyline series (where he averaged 56.57) impacted his average against England.

question: What is the most balls faced in a Test innings by a batsman?
explanation: Hanif Mohammad of Pakistan faced 777 balls in his innings of 337 against West Indies in Bridgetown in 1958, the most balls faced in a single Test innings in history.

question: Who was the bowler who dismissed Adam Gilchrist for a duck in the 2003 ODI World Cup final?
explanation: Zaheer Khan dismissed Adam Gilchrist for a duck in the 2003 ODI World Cup final in Johannesburg. Despite this breakthrough, Australia went on to score 359/2 (with Ponting's 140*) and won convincingly.

question: What was the exact margin of victory for West Indies in the 1975 Cricket World Cup final?
explanation: West Indies defeated Australia by 17 runs in the 1975 Cricket World Cup final at Lord's. Vivian Richards' run-out (3 run-outs in total from Richards) was decisive in skittling Australia.

question: What scoring milestone did Jasprit Bumrah achieve during the 2024-25 Test series?
options: A. Became India's leading Test wicket-taker
B. Reached ICC No. 1 ranking in Tests while also becoming the top-ranked T20I bowler
C. Took 200 Test wickets at the fastest pace for India
D. Was ranked No. 1 Test bowler for a record consecutive months
explanation: Jasprit Bumrah was ranked as the world's No. 1 Test bowler for a record number of consecutive months, cementing his status as the best Test bowler in the world in the 2024-25 period.

question: What is the only ICC full-member nation to have never been knocked out in the group stage of an ODI World Cup?
explanation: Australia has an extraordinary record of never having been eliminated at the group stage of an ODI World Cup. They have consistently qualified from the group stage in every edition they have played.
